LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY DEWITT'S RE-NOTICED MOTION TO TRANSFER AND CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT TRANSFER AND COORDINATION IS WARRANTED OR APPROPRIATE UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTIONS 403 AND 404.1. COMMON QUESTIONS OF LAW OR FACT DO NOT SUFFICIENTLY PREDOMINATE OVER THE TWO ACTIONS. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT HE WOULD BE DISADVANTAGED BY INCONSISTENT RULINGS IN THESE DIFFERENT CASES. COORDINATION DOES NOT BENEFIT THE CONVENIENCE OF THE PARTIES, WITNESSES, AND COUNSEL SUFFICIENT TO TRANSFER A CASE FROM ALAMEDA COUNTY TO THIS COURT. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT COORDINATING THE CASES WOULD SERVE THE EFFICIENT UTILIZATION OF JUDICIAL FACILITIES AND RESOURCES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside March 16, 2015 Default
Off Calendar May-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT IAC/INTERACTIVECORP's Motion To Set Aside March 16, 2015 Default is granted. The summons does not state that Defendant is sued under a fictitious name, as required by CCP sec. 474. Accordingly, the Court does not have personal jurisdiction over defendant. (Armstrong v. Sup. Ct. (1956) 144 Cal.App.2d 420, 424.) The default is therefore void and set aside pursuant to CCP sec. 473(d). (See Carol Gilbert, Inc. v. Haller (2009) 179 Cal.App.4th 852.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/LOT)
